rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE SHIRLEY OUTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/27/2025, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No waiver of account signed by Royal National Institute for the Blind (RNIB) as personal representative of the sole beneficiary (Mabel Cocking) is on file. 2) The current bond ($716,000) is insufficient in light of the anticipated distribution from the decedent's trust. The bond must be increased, or RNIB as the beneficiary's personal representative must sign a waiver of additional bond. 3) A certified copy of letters of administration issued to RNIB as Mabel Cocking's personal representative must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). An original apostille must be attached to the certified copy. The certification must be made within 60 days before the hearing. See LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(6).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GAVIN LUM SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TRANSFER to SACRAMENTO County
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/27/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee ordered on 12/14/22 in the conservatorship (not "limited") case must be filed before the trust matter can be transferred. (2) There was no Exhibit A attached to the supplement filed on 11/6/24. (3) There is nothing in either the SNT or the order approving the SNT that specifies that "only" two TAC members are required; the parents are identified as initial members, but not the ONLY members. It is also untrue that there is no process to appoint additional members. Article Five, paragraph A says that the TAC "shall consist of a minimum of threemembers," and paragraphs F and G provide that a member of the TAC may appoint his/her successor "so that there will be at least three members serving at all times." Two provisions requiring three TAC members do not appear to be a typo. (Article Two, Section 5, however, does appear to have a typo, in which the beneficiary is referred to in the feminine) (4) Since it was this Court's order approving the SNT, this Court should amend its own order, if appropriate. (5) Please file a copy of the parties' consents to representation by the same attorney, which clearly indicates that they are aware of the potential conflicts. If the consents were previously filed, please indicate when they were filed (or to which pleading they were attached). Please provide a courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/14/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The bank's trust department has always been in Sacramento County for the first 8 accountings already filed in this case. Parties have been able to appear via Zoom since 2020. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, but only because the administrator is the sole heir. Requirements for a report of status, as set forth in LRSF 14.35 J., were not met. Hearing will be set for 10/6/25 for status of filing either a petition for final distribution or a third report of status that fully addresses the status of administration. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] The hearing on 4/8/24 was not for this second "status report" but for status of closing the estate. (This second "status report" was not filed until 9/23/24.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MANUEL MINERO AND VINCENTA MINERO LIVING TRUST, AS AMENDED MARCH 1, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition does not appear to be ready for the Court's ruling at this time because the proposed distribution is not even based on the parties' agreement. In that case, the petition does not provide authorities under or grounds on which the court can grant the relief as presented without determining whether the 2005 Trust or the 2007 amendment should be valid and/or operative terms. The petition merely asks for the court to affirm the petitioners' decision as to how to divide the assets without proper reasoning. Procedural issues will be further reviewed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/15/25. (No earlier date available.) 1. Still no proof of publication has been filed, showing that the petitioner seeks to have a WILL admitted. 2. The waivers of bond filed on 6/24/24 and 7/1/24 were NOT new, complete waivers of bond, showing the name of the proposed personal representative in item 4; the dates were exactly the same as on the incomplete waivers of bond filed on 1/17/24 and 1/22/24. 3. The successor trustee was appointed on 10/30/24. He must also waive bond and must receive or waive notice. Deadline to deliver courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of the proof of publication and the correct, complete, and current waivers of bond is 12/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEVEN BRYAN JEFFE LIVING TRUST DTD JUNE 9, 2017
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Title To Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) How is this the proper venue? It appears that the successor trustee resides in Alameda County. [Probate Code Sect. 17002.] (2) The incorrect form of notice was served: DE-120 instead of DE-115. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (5) Is Sophia Helen Jeffe still under age 24? If so, the contingent residuary beneficiaries must be included in the list of beneficiaries and must receive (or waive) notice. If the petition is not withdrawn before the continued hearing date, please provide courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers, including any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/31/24. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRIETTA WEBBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the telephone number was not included in the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities. Prior to filing a petition for final distribution, Administrator must file a petition, pursuant to Probate Code Section 11700 for the Court to determine who is enttitled to inherit. Status of closing the estate will be set for 12/8/25. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 12/8/25 if no petition has been filed. (The two copies of Letters provided to the court were not signed.)

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA A. NOBLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The original will does not appear to have been lodged yet. [Probate Code Sect. 8200(a)(1).] (2) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (3) Neither the mailed nor published notice indicated that the petitioner seeks to have a will admitted. (4) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/31/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD GEORGE STROH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/24. The following issues must be cleared two weeks prior to the date: 1. The original Will has not been submitted. 2. Proof of Subscribing Witness to be filed. Petition #3.f.(2) affirms that the decedent's will is self-proving; however, it is not as the witnesses' attestation does not meet CCP 2015.5. 3. Petition #6 to affirm the first box that applies, regarding information by whom the decedent was survived. Petition left #6 blank. Heirs-at-law must be listed on Petition #8, and they must be properly noticed. LRSF 14.18.C.1) must be met. 4. Proof of publication to be filed. 5. Petition seeks the funds be deposited into blocked account(s); however, the court may rather require bond. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAULANN JOY DYMOND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/24. The following issues need to be cleared two weeks prior. (1) Petition #8 to identify if the nephew (Benjamin H. Isaac) is a child of the petitioner or another sibling of the decedent. Petition to identify the ancestor(s) under the second generation (nephew) to take. LRSF 14.18.C.3). (2) Petition #8 to identify if a parent of each niece of the decedent's predeceased spouse. Petition to identify the ancestor(s) under the second generation (nieces) to take. LRSF 14.18.C.3). (3) NEED the dates of the death of the decedent's parents. LRSF 14.18.C.3). (4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] to be filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E and 14.59.C. (5) Proof of service of Notice of Petition to Administer Estate needs to have an accurate date signed by the server/counsel. (6) No proposed order has been submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
